We never spam.", "relevance_score": 0.6}, {"title": "Crouching Rivals, Not-So-Hidden Dragon: SpaceX and the Future of Launch ...", "url": "https://londoneconomics.co.uk/blog/publication/crouching-rivals-not-so-hidden-dragon-spacex-and-the-future-of-launch-competition-part-1/", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content] Title: Crouching Rivals, Not-So-Hidden Dragon: SpaceX and the Future of Launch Competition - Part 1 - London Economics URL Source: https://londoneconomics.co.uk/blog/publication/crouching-rivals-not-so-hidden-dragon-spacex-and-the-future-of-launch-competition-part-1/ Markdown Content: | Practice area: | [Space](https://londoneconomics.co.uk/blog/sector/space/ \"Space\")[](https://londoneconomics.co.uk/blog/publication/crouching-rivals-not-so-hidden-dragon-spacex-and-the-future-of-launch-competition-part-1/) | | --- | | Client: | N/A | | Published: | 18 September, 2024 | | Keywords: | [Launch](https://londoneconomics.co.uk/blog/tag/launch/)[Monopoly](https://londoneconomics.co.uk/blog/tag/monopoly/)[Space in Focus](https://londoneconomics.co.uk/blog/tag/space-in-focus/)[SpaceX](https://londoneconomics.co.uk/blog/tag/spacex/)[Starship](https://londoneconomics.co.uk/blog/tag/starship/) | **_In light of SpaceX\u2019s recent uncharacteristic setbacks and Boeing\u2019s ongoing challenges, the lack of a strong spaceflight competitor has never been more evident. The industry\u2019s heavy reliance on a single provider raises concerns, yet the current landscape shows little sign of shifting. This first Space in Focus instalment of a two-part series on an outlook of the launch industry details the factors contributing to SpaceX\u2019s market dominance. It explores how the company\u2019s business model has created a formidable competitive advantage, presenting significant hurdles for rivals attempting to close the gap._** Resilience has emerged as a central theme in the space launch industry, exemplified by two examples of launch vulnerabilities this year: SpaceX\u2019s Falcon 9 anomaly in July 2024[[1]](https://londoneconomics.co.uk/blog/publication/crouching-rivals-not-so-hidden-dragon-spacex-and-the-future-of-launch-competition-part-1/#_ftn1) and the Boeing Starliner saga, spanning June until August 2024[[2]](https://londoneconomics.co.uk/blog/publication/crouching-rivals-not-so-hidden-dragon-spacex-and-the-future-of-launch-competition-part-1/#_ftn2). While similar, the responses differed markedly. SpaceX managed to recover quickly from the Falcon 9 incident, resuming operations within two weeks[[3]](https://londoneconomics.co.uk/blog/publication/crouching-rivals-not-so-hidden-dragon-spacex-and-the-future-of-launch-competition-part-1/#_ftn3). In contrast, the Boeing Starliner situation persisted for over a month. NASA ultimately opted to extend the astronauts\u2019 mission from the planned 8 days to 8 months, arranging their return on a SpaceX Crew Dragon instead of the Starliner[[4]](https://londoneconomics.co.uk/blog/publication/crouching-rivals-not-so-hidden-dragon-spacex-and-the-future-of-launch-competition-part-1/#_ftn4). What was expected to introduce a serious competitor to SpaceX in human spaceflight has instead highlighted the weakness of alternatives. SpaceX\u2019s dominance of the space industry is so pronounced, that it can be visualised in a single graph[[5]](https://londoneconomics.", "relevance_score": 0.85}, {"title": "Starfish Space raises $110M to boost satellite servicing missions ...", "url": "https://www.geekwire.com/2026/starfish-space-raises-more-than-100m-to-scale-up-its-satellite-servicing-missions/", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content] Title: Starfish Space raises $110M to scale up its satellite servicing missions URL Source: https://www.geekwire.com/2026/starfish-space-raises-more-than-100m-to-scale-up-its-satellite-servicing-missions/ Published Time: 2026-04-07T13:00:00+00:00 Markdown Content: # Starfish Space raises $110M to boost satellite servicing missions \u2013 GeekWire We use essential cookies to make our site work.
